text:
Market today: Mixed but leaning cautiously optimistic. The U.S.-Iran pause and Hormuz reopening is taking geopolitical tail-risk off the table for now, which helps sentiment. But the real story for your book is headline #10 — data center credibility is under pressure, and that's the core narrative holding up your chip/AI names.

Your holdings: AVGO is the urgent one — down 16% and your largest position, on a SELL signal. On margin, that hole gets deeper fast. PLTR is also on SELL, down 13% — momentum names bleed hard when sentiment cracks. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; even a small adverse move is unpredictable. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: 1) Whether the data center narrative stabilizes or keeps deteriorating — that's the linchpin for AVGO, SMH, and CRWD. 2) SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF already down 17% — decay plus leverage is a silent killer, check your exit daily.

Bottom line: Your two biggest SELL flags are bleeding on margin — AVGO especially demands a decision today, not tomorrow.
